sections.title: Why this overlay exists
sections.summary: Temper needs a stack overlay for web-first games that split client, services, simulation, and content tooling across packages. This stack is common for: - browser RPGs - on-chain o

sections.title: Structural doctrine
sections.summary: Prefer: - one package for the player client - one package for backend or service surfaces - one package for simulation or contract/runtime logic where applicable - root scripts tha

sections.title: Source-of-truth implications
sections.summary: For this stack, the repo should make it obvious which paths are: - canonical game content - generated code - client projections of content - deploy and environment surfaces Generat

sections.title: Shipping implications
sections.summary: `ship lite` can often stay at the root script layer. `ship full` should usually add: - client typecheck - package-specific smoke or simulation passes - release-note generation from

sections.title: Failure modes
sections.summary: - client data copies drift from canonical content - package-level scripts diverge from root workflow - deploy knowledge only exists in one service package - assistant advice ignore

sections.title: Best Temper pairings
sections.summary: - `Kaplan 🎯` for player trust and onboarding - `Carmack 🔧` for build and deploy integrity - `Miyamoto ✨` for first-session client feel
